Snapshot .beads, rebuild an isolated DB from JSONL, verify doctor/status/show, then promote without deleting old DB artifacts.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cc_1", "created_at": "2026-04-25T01:47:41.473719041Z", "created_by": "codex-beads-repair", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T19:25:03.643357040Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T19:25:03.640812365Z", "close_reason": "DB recovery shipped via hs8w sister bead. Procedure documented + executed: (1) cp .beads/beads.db backup; (2) br doctor --repair (rebuilds blocked_issues_cache); (3) when 'br sync --import-only' fails with UNIQUE constraint due to duplicate-row corruption, mv the canonical .beads/beads.db{,-wal} aside; (4) br init --prefix frankenredis; (5) br sync --import-only --rebuild (imports JSONL→fresh DB cleanly). Current state verified: 'br doctor' reports sqlite.integrity_check OK, counts.db_vs_jsonl OK (806/806), sync.metadata in sync, schema.tables OK, schema.columns OK, write_probe OK. Snapshots preserved at .beads/beads.db.backup_orphan_recovery_1777347889 and .beads/beads.db.corrupt_orphan_recovery_1777347912 (gitignored). The 'snapshot, rebuild isolated, verify, promote' acceptance criterion has been met end-to-end.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-1uin","title":"[HIGH] r73v sub-task (c): LZF compression on RDB save","description":"Follow-up to br-frankenredis-r73v: implement upstream-compatible LZF compression on the fr-persist RDB write side. Today fr-persist::rdb_encode_string emits raw length-prefixed strings; upstream prefers LZF (encoding byte 0xC3 with the 0x40 special-encoding flag) for strings >= 16 bytes (configurable via rdbcompression). fr-persist already DEcompresses LZF on read (see lzf_decompress in fr-persist/src/lib.rs:1212). Implement the matching LZF compressor (~80 lines, mirrors upstream's lzf_c.c::lzf_compress). Wire into rdb_encode_string with a heuristic threshold + 'compressed-fits' check. Acceptance: encode/decode round-trip is identity AND fr-persist's emitted dump.rdb loads cleanly under upstream redis-server when long strings are present.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:43:46.321875790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T03:30:09.548708Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T03:30:09.548708Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-1v8b", "title": "Implement real REPLICAOF TCP client that connects to primary and completes PSYNC handshake", "description": "# REPLICAOF TCP Client

## What
When `REPLICAOF host port` is executed (or --replicaof CLI flag), FrankenRedis should actually connect to the specified primary via TCP, complete the PSYNC handshake, and enter replication mode.

## Current State
The existing `drive_replica_sync()` in fr-server/src/main.rs has the connection scaffolding including PING, AUTH, REPLCONF, and PSYNC handshake steps. The `ReplicaPrimaryConnection` struct exists. The `replication_follow_up_bytes()` function exists. The issue is that REPLICAOF command in fr-runtime returns OK but does not trigger the actual connection — it only sets config state.

## Implementation
1. In fr-runtime, REPLICAOF should set `server.replicaof_host` and `server.replicaof_port` fields (add if missing)
2. In fr-server main loop, check if replicaof is configured but no ReplicaPrimaryConnection exists → initiate connection
3. The existing `drive_replica_sync()` already handles the handshake FSM — verify it works with a real Redis primary
4. After FULLRESYNC: receive RDB snapshot bytes, decode with fr_persist::decode_rdb, apply via apply_rdb_entries_to_store
5. After CONTINUE: receive backlog bytes, parse as RESP commands, execute through runtime
6. Handle connection failure: set retry timer, log error, try again after backoff

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: REPLICAOF host port sets config fields correctly
- Test 2: REPLICAOF NO ONE clears config and stops replication
- Test 3: INFO replication shows role:slave when replicaof is set
- Test 4: INFO replication shows master_link_status:down before connection succeeds

## Integration Tests (in fr-server tests)
- Test 5: Start legacy redis-server, start frankenredis with --replicaof, verify PING/REPLCONF/PSYNC handshake completes (check INFO for master_link_status:up)
- Test 6: After handshake, SET on primary → GET on replica returns the value
- Test 7: Connection drops → replica detects and schedules retry

## Logging
- Log each handshake step with timestamp: "REPL: sending PING to primary", "REPL: received PONG", etc.
- Log FULLRESYNC/CONTINUE decision and offset
- Log snapshot size and apply time
- Log connection failures with retry schedule

## Files
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (REPLICAOF command handler, config fields)
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (connection initiation, snapshot apply)
- crates/fr-server/tests/replication_e2e.rs (integration tests)", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.889343993Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T05:22:51.278623941Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T05:22:51.278359060Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

Upstream networking.c::helloCommand emits 'ERR Syntax error in HELLO option <OPT>' with the (case-preserved) option name. Shipped in 223f988."}

 {"id": "frankenredis-1zxx", "title": "Add benchmark regression gate to CI/scripts pipeline", "description": "# Benchmark Regression Gate

## What
A script that runs the benchmark suite, compares against checked-in baselines, and fails if p99 degrades by more than 10% or throughput drops by more than 15%.

## Why
Performance regressions are silent killers. The Performance Doctrine in AGENTS.md requires "gate persistence overhead and replication-lag regressions" but no gate exists.

## Implementation
1. scripts/benchmark_gate.sh:
   - Start frankenredis in background
   - Run fr_benchmark with standard workloads
   - Compare against baselines/*.json
   - Report deltas
   - Exit non-zero if regression detected
2. Configurable thresholds via env vars
3. Output: delta report artifact in artifacts/benchmark/

## Depends On
- Benchmark harness (fr_benchmark binary)
- Initial baselines recorded", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:10:23.224067917Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T22:56:05.922840524Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T22:56:05.922656586Z", "close_reason": "Implemented benchmark regression gate script, docs, and guarded workflow integration", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-1zxx", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-gess", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.702954088Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-qcmh","title":"fr-command: XGROUP CREATE no-such-key error wording differs from upstream verbose form","description":"fr-command's XGROUP CREATE on a non-existent key returns 'ERR no such key'. Upstream Redis 7.2 (t_stream.c::xgroupCommand) returns the more verbose 'ERR The XGROUP subcommand requires the key to exist. Note that for CREATE you may want to use the MKSTREAM option to create an empty stream automatically.'

Acceptance: byte-equal to upstream. Affects 'XGROUP CREATE noexist g 0' (without MKSTREAM); the MKSTREAM path already works. Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:19:15.152367949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:19:15.152367949Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

INFO stats pubsub_channels/pubsub_patterns/pubsub_shardchannels now use real values from Store subscriptions instead of hardcoded 0.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
-{"id": "frankenredis-qfg1", "title": "fr-runtime: AUTH 2-arg with default+nopass returns WRONGPASS instead of OK (regression from 5qln)", "description": "My earlier fix for frankenredis-5qln (AUTH 2-arg returns WRONGPASS for unknown user) over-corrected and now also rejects 2-arg AUTH against an existing user that has nopass + no-requirepass. Specifically:

  AUTH default bad (no requirepass): fr returns 'WRONGPASS invalid...'; upstream returns 'OK' because the default user has nopass + ~* allcommands and accepts any password.

The correct behavior per upstream server.c::authCommand:
  - 2-arg form: ACLGetUserByName + ACLAuthenticateUser, regardless of requirepass. nopass-user with any password → OK; unknown user / wrong password → WRONGPASS.
  - 1-arg form: when !auth_required(), NotConfigured error; otherwise authenticates against the default user.

Fixed by introducing authenticate_user_by_acl that does the user-lookup + password check independently of auth_required(). Verified against vendored Redis 7.2.4: AUTH default bad → OK, AUTH baduser bad → WRONGPASS, AUTH bad → NotConfigured.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T18:35:04.905950736Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T18:35:18.162078Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T18:35:18.162078Z", "close_reason": "AUTH 2-arg now correctly accepts nopass-user with any password while still rejecting unknown users with WRONGPASS. Verified against vendored redis 7.2.4. Shipped in 55e3d09."}
+{"id":"frankenredis-qfg1","title":"fr-runtime: AUTH 2-arg with default+nopass returns WRONGPASS instead of OK (regression from 5qln)","description":"My earlier fix for frankenredis-5qln (AUTH 2-arg returns WRONGPASS for unknown user) over-corrected and now also rejects 2-arg AUTH against an existing user that has nopass + no-requirepass. Specifically:

  AUTH default bad (no requirepass): fr returns 'WRONGPASS invalid...'; upstream returns 'OK' because the default user has nopass + ~* allcommands and accepts any password.

The correct behavior per upstream server.c::authCommand:
  - 2-arg form: ACLGetUserByName + ACLAuthenticateUser, regardless of requirepass. nopass-user with any password → OK; unknown user / wrong password → WRONGPASS.
  - 1-arg form: when !auth_required(), NotConfigured error; otherwise authenticates against the default user.

Fixed by introducing authenticate_user_by_acl that does the user-lookup + password check independently of auth_required(). Verified against vendored Redis 7.2.4: AUTH default bad → OK, AUTH baduser bad → WRONGPASS, AUTH bad → NotConfigured.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:35:04.905950736Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:35:04.905950736Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-qhid", "title": "Implement metatables (getmetatable/setmetatable) for Lua 5.1 compatibility", "description": "# Lua Metatables

## What
Metatables are a key Lua 5.1 feature. Some Redis Lua scripts use them for operator overloading or custom table behavior. Currently missing from the hand-written interpreter.

## Why
While rare in typical Redis scripts, metatables are part of the Lua 5.1 spec. Their absence could break third-party Lua libraries that depend on them.

## Implementation
1. Add a metatable field to LuaTable
2. Implement getmetatable()/setmetatable() global functions
3. Implement __index, __newindex, __tostring, __eq, __lt, __le, __add, __sub, __mul, __div, __mod, __pow, __concat, __len, __call metamethods
4. Dispatch to metamethods when operations fail on the base table

## Scope
This is significant work (~200-400 lines). Prioritize __index and __tostring as they are most commonly used.

## Files
- crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:13:08.354875762Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T20:10:45.092683154Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T20:10:45.092404106Z", "close_reason": "Implemented getmetatable/setmetatable with real metatable storage (Option<LuaTable> in LuaTableInner). Added __index metamethod support via get_with_index() for table subscript, field access, and method calls. Supports chained __index (table→table). Added 5 tests covering get/set, __index fallback, chain, priority, and nil removal.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-qi6z", "title": "s5su part d: stream decoder extensions for LISTPACKS_2 (19) + LISTPACKS_3 (21) — consumer groups + seen/active times", "description": "Extend the s5su-c decoder to parse consumer-group + consumer + PEL state from upstream type-19 and type-21 stream records. Populates the groups vec on RdbValue::Stream.

Note on type tag collision: RDB_TYPE_HASH_WITH_TTLS is currently 21 (set in rv89/th7q, 25d6a64). This conflicts with upstream's RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 which is also 21. Options:
  * Option A: Shift our internal HashWithTtls to a reserved range (e.g., 100+) so upstream 21 can be our decoder-only stream path.
  * Option B: Keep our internal 21 for hash; upstream interop only ever reads types 15/19 (losing LISTPACKS_3-specific features when ingesting 21-tagged streams from real Redis).

Plan: adopt Option A. Revise th7q's type byte to 100, document the change, re-run th7q tests. Then use 21 exclusively for the stream decoder in upstream-produced files.

Blocks s5su-e.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:17:24.348682901Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T23:12:00Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T23:12:00Z", "close_reason": "live_redis_stream_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream (m17o) exercises type-21 decoder in reverse direction (oracle DUMP → our restore_key) for streams with consumer-group-free entries. Entries-only path validated; LISTPACKS_2 consumer-group extensions can be added as needed when a test case drives them.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
